rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by tracing invalid identifiers or helper declarations and resolving root causes or removing invalid clauses.
Move implementation-level bindings from component type annexes to implementation annexes when diagnostics reference subcomponent paths or implementation-specific features.
Avoid generating AGREE clauses that reference opaque Boolean data or perform numeric comparisons on non-scalar types unless the requirement and AADL model support such operations.
generic_rules: Ensure AGREE contracts only reference visible identifiers and features within the current scope to avoid diagnostics related to undefined or inaccessible symbols.
Validate that all property associations and data type references in AGREE contracts are present in the AADL model and its configured dependencies.
When generating AGREE guarantees, ensure they are placed in the correct annex scope (type vs. implementation) based on the referenced features and bindings.
Remove or revise AGREE clauses that reference invalid or unsupported data types, such as Boolean ports used in numeric comparisons.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks and placed in appropriate annex scopes (type or implementation).
AGREE guarantees referencing component ports must be in valid scopes; implementation-level bindings should not appear in component type annexes.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers in AGREE code unless they are explicitly defined in the AADL model or its dependencies.
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to resolve diagnostics by adding new architecture elements; instead, correct or remove invalid AGREE clauses.
Avoid placing implementation-specific references in component type annexes, as this leads to scope-related validation errors.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about property associations not pointing to anything
Model fusion and owner/scope preservation rules
